title: senecio subauriculatus
intro: Senecio subauriculatus, also known as the cotton bush or round-leaved groundsel, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its small white or yellow flowers and cotton-like seeds. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, the plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Senecio subauriculatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Senecio subauriculatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
